scope: B group second-round forecast audit and parameter updates for future match modeling.

audit.score: major miss; blowout tail severely underweighted
audit.total_goals: small-score script failed after Qatar discipline collapse
audit.script: Canada home pressure script hit; Qatar low-block resilience and discipline were overestimated

audit.direction: missed draw lean; Switzerland win probability too low
audit.score: major miss; late Swiss burst and red-card tail were underweighted
audit.script: Swiss control was correctly identified, but conversion and substitution impact were over-penalized

parameter_updates.reason: Canada's home press, speed and vertical attack created a far higher ceiling than modeled despite Davies-related concerns.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Canada home matches against low-block or high-discipline-risk opponents
parameter_updates.round3_constraint: Model Ismael Kone injury separately before applying full value.

parameter_updates.reason: Qatar's red cards turned a Canada-favored match into a 6-0 blowout; the model had not amplified 4+ goal tails enough.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Canada leading against a team reduced to ten or nine men, especially before 60 minutes

parameter_updates.reason: Two red cards under sustained pressure show a much higher collapse-risk profile.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Qatar against high-press, high-speed opponents or when trailing

parameter_updates.new: -0.05 when trailing or down a player
parameter_updates.reason: The first-round draw with Switzerland did not generalize once Qatar conceded and lost discipline.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Qatar after first concession, red card, or forced open-game state

parameter_updates.reason: Johan Manzambi and Ruben Vargas changed the match state; Switzerland's late bench impact was materially underestimated.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Switzerland in stalemate games after 60 minutes

parameter_updates.new: 0 baseline; +0.10 xG if opponent opens up or loses a player
parameter_updates.reason: The first-round draw with Qatar caused an excessive conversion penalty; Switzerland converted control into goals in round two.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Switzerland controlling possession/territory, especially after opponent fatigue or discipline events

parameter_updates.reason: Muharemovic's red card preceded a rapid Swiss scoring burst and structural collapse.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Bosnia and Herzegovina trailing, fatigued, or down a player against a control team

parameter_updates.reason: The model over-carried Bosnia's first-round draw resistance into a match where late Swiss pressure broke the game.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Bosnia and Herzegovina against stronger control teams, especially after 60 minutes

parameter_updates.reason: Two first-round 1-1 draws did not imply another draw-heavy round; second-round pressure created open tails.
parameter_updates.applies_to: B group round three and future group-specific priors

parameter_updates.reason: Canada and Switzerland both produced heavy wins after favorable match-state swings.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Clear favorites with early lead, speed/bench advantage, or opponent discipline collapse

parameter_updates.reason: The model was materially below market on Canada and Switzerland win probabilities, while the market direction was correct.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Clear favorites where market and team-strength fundamentals agree and lineup gaps are not severe

model_lessons: First-round group-wide draws should not mechanically inflate the next round's draw baseline.
Red cards must be treated as scoreline-tail multipliers, not just small win-probability adjustments.
Canada's home attacking ceiling remains high even with Davies-related left-side concerns.
Switzerland's late substitutions and control-to-goal conversion need a conditional uplift rather than a blanket finishing discount.
Qatar and Bosnia and Herzegovina both showed discipline/collapse risk once their defensive plan was disrupted.
handoff_to_round3: Canada vs Switzerland must include Ismael Kone injury uncertainty and possible market overreaction to two big favorite wins.
Qatar and Bosnia and Herzegovina require discipline, suspension and morale checks before final-round modeling.
B group draw baseline should reset downward from the round-one all-draw prior.
